volumeTitle: Casina. The Casket Comedy. Curculio. Epidicus. The Two Menaechmuses
label: Casina (Section 5.2.906b - Section 5.4.994c)

sections.label: Section 5.2.906b
sections.translation: Babae !

sections.label: Section 5.2.906c
sections.translation: Quid ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.906d
sections.translation: Papae !

sections.label: Section 5.2.907
sections.translation: ⋯ quid est ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.907b
sections.translation: Oh , erat maximum . Gladium ne haberet metui ; id quaerere occœpi . Dum , gladiumne habeat , quaero , arripio capulum .

sections.label: Section 5.2.910
sections.original: séd cum cogitó , non habuit gládium , nam esset frígidus .
sections.translation: Sed , quom cogito , non habuit gladium ; nam id esset frigidius .

sections.label: Section 5.2.911
sections.translation: Eloquere .

sections.label: Section 5.2.911b
sections.translation: At pudet .

sections.label: Section 5.2.911c
sections.translation: Num radix fuit ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.911d
sections.translation: Non fuit .

sections.label: Section 5.2.911e
sections.translation: Num cucumis ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.912
sections.original: Profecto hercle ⋯ non fuit quicquam holerum ,
sections.translation: Profecto hercle non fuit quidquam olerum ; Nisi quidquid erat , calamitas profecto attigerat nunquam . Ita , quidquid erat , grande erat .

sections.label: Section 5.2.915
sections.translation: Quid fit denique ? Edisserta .

sections.label: Section 5.2.915b
sections.translation: When I addressed Casina , Casina , said I , my dear wife , why do you slight your husband in this fashion ? Really , upon my faith , you do this quite without my deserving it , inasmuch as I have given you the preference as my wife .

sections.label: Section 5.2.921
sections.original: ílla haud verbum fácit et saepit véste id qui estis múlieres .
sections.translation: She answered not a word .

sections.label: Section 5.2.925
sections.translation: Surge , ut ineam in ⋯ Atque illam in ⋯

sections.label: Section 5.2.927
sections.translation: Perlepide narrat ⋯

sections.label: Section 5.2.928
sections.translation: When I attempted a kiss , a beard pricked my lips just like briars .

sections.label: Section 5.2.930
sections.original: contínuo in genua ut ástiti , pectús mihi pedibus pércutit .
sections.translation: Forthwith , as I was upon my knees , she struck my head with her feet . I tumbled headlong from the bed ; she leapt down upon me and punched my face . From there in silence out of doors I came in this guise ; by your leaves I say it ; may the old fellow drink of the same cup that I have been drinking of .

sections.label: Section 5.2.933b
sections.translation: Most excellent . But where ’ s your cloak ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.934b
sections.translation: I left it here in-doors .

sections.label: Section 5.2.935
sections.original: Quid núnc ? satin lepide adita est vobís manus ?
sections.translation: Well now ; hasn ’ t a very nice trick been played you ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.935b
sections.translation: Yes , and deservedly . Hush ! the door makes a noise . What , is she following me , I wonder ? ( They go to a distance .)

sections.label: Section 5.3.937
sections.original: Máxumo ego ardeo flágitio néc quid agam meis rébus scio ,
sections.translation: I ’ m branded with the greatest disgrace , nor what , under my circumstances to do , do I know . Nor yet how to look my wife in the face ; so utterly undone am I ! All my misdeeds are discovered .

sections.label: Section 5.3.940
sections.translation: In every way , to my confusion , I am ruined ! So clearly am I hooked fast by the jaws ! nor know I in what way to clear myself before my wife ; wretch that I am , to have been stripped of my cloak ! ⋯ These clandestine nuptials are all discovered .

sections.label: Section 5.3.945
sections.translation: ⋯ I judge it best for me ⋯ She taught my wife ⋯ the way ⋯ But who is there , what person would be ready to undertake this office

sections.label: Section 5.3.950
sections.original: pró me ? quid núnc agam néscio , nisi ut ímprobos
sections.translation: for me ? What now to do I know not , except to imitate worthless slaves , and fly from the house ; for there ’ s no safety for my shoulder-blades if I return home . I may tell lies there ; i ’ faith , I shall get a basting , though much against my will , although I have earned my punishment .

sections.label: Section 5.3.955
sections.original: hac dabo protinam me et fugiam .
sections.translation: I ’ ll at once betake myself in this direction in flight .

sections.label: Section 5.3.955b
sections.translation: Hallo there ! Stop , this instant , you amorous one !

sections.label: Section 5.3.956_962
sections.original: Óccidi , revocór : quasi non aúdiam , abíbo .
sections.translation: I ’ m utterly undone ! I ’ m being called back . I ’ ll be off , as though I didn ’ t hear .

sections.label: Section 5.4.963
sections.original: Úbi tu es , qui cólere mores Mássilienses póstulas ?
sections.translation: Where are you , you who imitate the morals of the Massilians [*] ? Now , if you wish to be taking liberties with me , is a good opportunity

sections.label: Section 5.4.965
sections.original: redi sis in cubiculum ; periisti hercle . age , accede huc modo .
sections.translation: [ ⋯ at your risk . By my troth , you are undone . Come , only step this way . ⋯ Now I fancy that when a witness out of ⋯ I ’ ll find ⋯ thus out of the street I order ⋯ a murmur I ⋯

sections.label: Section 5.4.970
sections.original: nunc ego intér sacrum saxumque sum nec quo fugiam scio .
sections.translation: Now am I in extreme danger , between the stone and the sacrifice , nor know I which way to fly ⋯ The wolf-dogs ⋯ it was ⋯

sections.label: Section 5.4.972
sections.original: hercle opinor permutabo ego illuc nunc verbum vetus :
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I do think ⋯ old there now like new .]

sections.label: Section 5.4.973
sections.original: hac ibo , caninam scaevam spero meliorem fore .
sections.translation: I ’ ll go this way . I trust that the omen of a bitch ’ s barking will prove the better [*] .

sections.label: Section 5.4.974b
sections.original: Mi vir , unde hoc ornatu advenis ?
sections.translation: What are you doing , my husband , my good man ? Whence come you in this guise ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.975
sections.original: quid fecisti scipione aut quod habuisti pallium ?
sections.translation: What have you done with your walking-stick , or how disposed of the cloak you had ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.976
sections.original: In adulterio , dum moechissat Casinam , credo perdidit .
sections.translation: While he was playing his loving pranks with Casina , he lost it , I fancy .

sections.label: Section 5.4.977
sections.translation: Utterly undone !

sections.label: Section 5.4.977b
sections.translation: Shall we go to bed again ? I am Casina .

sections.label: Section 5.4.977c
sections.translation: Away with you to utter perdition !

sections.label: Section 5.4.978
sections.translation: Don ’ t you love me ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.978b
sections.original: Quin responde , tuo quid factum est pallio ?
sections.translation: Nay , but answer me ; what has become of your cloak ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.979
sections.translation: [ Upon my faith , wife , the Bacchantes !

sections.label: Section 5.4.979b
sections.translation: Bacchantes [*] !

sections.label: Section 5.4.979c
sections.translation: Bacchantes !

sections.label: Section 5.4.979d
sections.translation: He ’ s making pretence on purpose

sections.label: Section 5.4.980
sections.original: nam ecastor nunc Bacchae nullae ludunt .
sections.translation: for , upon my word , no Bacchantes are exhibiting at the present time .

sections.label: Section 5.4.980b
sections.translation: I forgot that . But still , the Bacchantes !—

sections.label: Section 5.4.981b
sections.translation: How , the Bacchantes ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.981c
sections.translation: Why , that cannot be .

sections.label: Section 5.4.982
sections.translation: By my troth , you are in a fright .

sections.label: Section 5.4.982b
sections.translation: What I ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.982c
sections.translation: I ’ faith , do tell no lies , for it ’ s quite clear . ⋯ ]

sections.label: Section 5.4.991
sections.original: qui étiam me miserum famosum fecit flagitiis suis .
sections.translation: [ ⋯ who even made a notorius man of me with his flagrant acts .] [*]

sections.label: Section 5.4.992
sections.translation: Won ’ t you hold your tongue ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.992b
sections.original: Non herle vero taceo . nam tu maxumo
sections.translation: I ’ troth , I certainly shall not hold my tongue ; for with the greatest earnestness you begged me to ask for Casina as my wife .

sections.label: Section 5.4.994_995
sections.translation: on account of my love for you .

sections.label: Section 5.4.994_995b
sections.translation: That I did

sections.label: Section 5.4.994c
sections.translation: I ’ faith , of her rather . ( Turning to CHALINUS .)
